rows.label: gallium
rows.title: Gallium
rows.dissipation_note: Bound in ICs, LEDs and thin-film PV — recoverable from fab scrap, but end-of-life collection is near zero.

rows.label: arsenic
rows.title: Arsenic
rows.dissipation_note: Dispersed in chemicals, glass and alloy additives; largely lost.

rows.label: hafnium
rows.title: Hafnium
rows.dissipation_note: In superalloys and nuclear parts — recoverable with the component, but volumes are tiny.

rows.label: helium
rows.title: Helium
rows.dissipation_note: Vents to the atmosphere in use — physically unrecoverable.

rows.label: germanium
rows.title: Germanium
rows.dissipation_note: Fibre and IR optics and electronics are recoverable; catalyst uses are dissipative.

rows.label: vanadium
rows.title: Vanadium
rows.dissipation_note: Most rides in steel and recycles with it; chemical/catalyst uses are lost.

rows.label: cobalt
rows.title: Cobalt
rows.dissipation_note: Batteries and superalloys are increasingly recycled.

rows.label: tantalum
rows.title: Tantalum
rows.dissipation_note: Capacitors are recyclable but dispersed and rarely collected.

rows.label: antimony
rows.title: Antimony
rows.dissipation_note: Lead-acid batteries recycle it; flame-retardant uses are dissipative.

rows.label: palladium
rows.title: Palladium
rows.dissipation_note: Autocatalysts and electronics are recovered efficiently.

rows.label: magnets
rows.title: Rare earths / magnets
rows.dissipation_note: NdFeB magnets are recyclable but collection is minimal.

rows.label: platinum
rows.title: Platinum
rows.dissipation_note: Autocatalysts are highly recycled; road-wear is a dissipative loss.

rows.label: titanium
rows.title: Titanium
rows.dissipation_note: Metal and alloys recycle; TiO2 pigment is dissipative.

rows.label: tungsten
rows.title: Tungsten
rows.dissipation_note: Hard-metal carbides are widely recycled.

rows.label: fluorspar
rows.title: Fluorspar
rows.dissipation_note: HF and chemical uses consume and disperse it.

rows.label: copper
rows.title: Copper
rows.dissipation_note: One of the best-recycled metals.

rows.label: nickel
rows.title: Nickel
rows.dissipation_note: Stainless steel and batteries recycle it.

rows.label: magnesium
rows.title: Magnesium
rows.dissipation_note: Alloys are recyclable.

rows.label: graphite
rows.title: Graphite
rows.dissipation_note: Battery-anode recycling is emerging; many uses are dissipative.

rows.label: niobium
rows.title: Niobium
rows.dissipation_note: Ferro-niobium recycles with steel.

rows.label: boron
rows.title: Boron
rows.dissipation_note: Glass, ceramics and fertiliser uses disperse it.

rows.label: lithium
rows.title: Lithium
rows.dissipation_note: Batteries — recycling is emerging and scalable.

rows.label: silicon
rows.title: Silicon
rows.dissipation_note: Metallurgical and chemical uses dissipate; some in recoverable products.

rows.label: feldspar
rows.title: Feldspar
rows.dissipation_note: Glass and ceramic uses disperse it.

rows.label: manganese
rows.title: Manganese
rows.dissipation_note: Recovered with steel.

rows.label: bauxite
rows.title: Bauxite / aluminium
rows.dissipation_note: Aluminium is highly recycled.

rows.label: phosphate
rows.title: Phosphate rock
rows.dissipation_note: Applied as fertiliser and dispersed into soil and water.

rows.label: baryte
rows.title: Baryte
rows.dissipation_note: Drilling mud and filler uses disperse it.

rows.label: beryllium
rows.title: Beryllium
rows.dissipation_note: Copper-beryllium alloys are recoverable; low volumes.

rows.label: strontium
rows.title: Strontium
rows.dissipation_note: Pyrotechnics, ferrite magnets and chemicals disperse it.

rows.label: phosphorus
rows.title: Phosphorus
rows.dissipation_note: Chemical and fertiliser uses disperse it irreversibly.
